reasoning: Strong evidence against return from multiple independent sources: (1) Hasina's son explicitly stated she will return "when elections are declared" (search_2_results_1,2,5,7), and (2) Interim government chief Muhammad Yunus confirmed elections possible only "by end of 2025" or "late 2025/early 2026" (search_2_results_0,8, search_2_summary). This definitively puts elections AFTER July 1, 2025, meaning her stated precondition cannot be met. (3) India shows "no inclination to extradite" and has given "no comment" for months despite formal December 2024 request (search_1_result_6, search_1_summary). (4) Expert consensus: former Indian diplomats state India is "unlikely to comply" due to political nature and treaty provisions allowing refusal (search_1_summary). (5) Extradition is complex judicial process requiring solid evidence and months-long coordination, not weeks (search_0_summary, search_1_summary). (6) Only 7 weeks remain from May 8 cutoff to July 1 resolution date. The combination of her own election condition unmet, India's unwillingness, and procedural complexity makes return before July 1 extremely unlikely (~5% probability).
